items.final_wording: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults
source.population: Adults with chronic cough lasting more than 8 weeks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist assessment of chronic cough.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult chronic cough assessment and referral; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

items.rationale: The exact BTS history section supports duration, impact, upper-airway and reflux-related context, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, and clinician-performed ENT or general assessment without attributing cough to an ENT cause.

items.final_wording: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults
source.population: Adults with chronic cough lasting more than 8 weeks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist assessment of chronic cough.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult chronic cough assessment and referral; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.

items.rationale: The exact investigation and referral section supports review of already available imaging or specialist reports, clinician concern, an entered plan, safety net, and arranged follow-up without ordering tests or referring automatically.
